arabic: ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَتَطْمَئِنُّ قُلُوبُهُم بِذِكْرِ ٱللَّهِ ۗ أَلَا بِذِكْرِ ٱللَّهِ تَطْمَئِنُّ ٱلْقُلُوبُ
translation: “Those who believe, and whose hearts find rest in the remembrance of God. Truly, in the remembrance of God do hearts find rest.”

sunni.label: Sunni emphasis
sunni.body: Sunni tafsir reads the remembrance as turning to God Himself, His names and His promise, and that calms an anxious heart.

shia.label: Shia emphasis
shia.body: Shia tafsir reads the remembrance as also pointing to the Prophet's Household: a narration names them the remembrance that brings the heart to rest.

voiceover.hook: What calms an anxious heart? This verse answers — and two traditions hear that remembrance differently.
voiceover.question: Which of these two readings resonates with you

cta_text: Which of these two readings resonates with you?
caption_desc: The cure for an anxious heart 🌙 #quran #tafsir #shia #sunni
